result.stdout:
2026-03-28 04:05:48.127 [ERRO] [DBEvoler]: Insufficient arguments.
DBEvoler is a database maintenance utility which can do the following:
1. Create a database
2. Migrate a database to the latest version

Syntax:
  dbevolver -t <database type> -c <connection string> -f <scripts folder> -v <version> -n <version tablename>

Parameters:
  -t: Valid values: SqlServer, Postgres, MySql.
  -c: Connection string including the database name.
  -f: Specifes the folder where database scripts are located. Optional. Default value is .\dbscripts
  -v: Specifies the version till which to upgrade the database. Optional. Default is to upgrade to the maximum version.
  -n: Specifies the name of the version table. Optional. Default is __Version_History__

SqlServer Examples:
  dbevolver -t SqlServer -c "Data Source=localhost; Initial Catalog=MyAppDb; User Id=TestUser; Password=password123; Integrated Security=False;"
  dbevolver -t SqlServer -c "Data Source=localhost; Initial Catalog=MyAppDb; User Id=TestUser; Password=password123; Integrated Security=False;" -n SchemaVersions
  dbevolver -t SqlServer -c "Data Source=localhost; Initial Catalog=MyAppDb; User Id=TestUser; Password=password123; Integrated Security=False;" -f .\scripts
  dbevolver -t SqlServer -c "Data Source=localhost; Initial Catalog=MyAppDb; User Id=TestUser; Password=password123; Integrated Security=False;" -v 101
  dbevolver -t SqlServer -c "Data Source=localhost; Initial Catalog=MyAppDb; User Id=TestUser; Password=password123; Integrated Security=False;" -f .\scripts
  dbevolver -t SqlServer -c "Data Source=localhost; Initial Catalog=MyAppDb; User Id=TestUser; Password=password123; Integrated Security=False;" -v 101 -f .\scripts
Postgres Examples:
  dbevolver -t Postgres -c "Server=localhost; Port=5432; Database=MyAppDb; UserId=postgres; Password=password123;"
  dbevolver -t Postgres -c "Server=localhost; Port=5432; Database=MyAppDb; UserId=postgres; Password=password123;" -n SchemaVersions
  dbevolver -t Postgres -c "Server=localhost; Port=5432; Database=MyAppDb; UserId=postgres; Password=password123;" -f .\scripts
  dbevolver -t Postgres -c "Server=localhost; Port=5432; Database=MyAppDb; UserId=postgres; Password=password123;" -v 101
  dbevolver -t Postgres -c "Server=localhost; Port=5432; Database=MyAppDb; UserId=postgres; Password=password123;" -f .\scripts
  dbevolver -t Postgres -c "Server=localhost; Port=5432; Database=MyAppDb; UserId=postgres; Password=password123;" -v 101 -f .\scripts
MySql Examples:
  dbevolver -t MySql -c "server=localhost; port=3306; uid=root; pwd=password123; database=MyAppDb"
  dbevolver -t MySql -c "server=localhost; port=3306; uid=root; pwd=password123; database=MyAppDb" -n SchemaVersions
  dbevolver -t MySql -c "server=localhost; port=3306; uid=root; pwd=password123; database=MyAppDb" -f .\scripts
  dbevolver -t MySql -c "server=localhost; port=3306; uid=root; pwd=password123; database=MyAppDb" -v 101
  dbevolver -t MySql -c "server=localhost; port=3306; uid=root; pwd=password123; database=MyAppDb" -f .\scripts
  dbevolver -t MySql -c "server=localhost; port=3306; uid=root; pwd=password123; database=MyAppDb" -v 101 -f .\scripts
